dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7498 IH: After Hours Child Care Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Hinson (for herself, Ms. Bonamici, Mr. Mackenzie, Mr. Pappas, Ms. Stefanik, and Mr. Pocan) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce

form.official-title: To establish and expand child care programs for parents who work nontraditional hours, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the After Hours Child Care Act.

section.header: Child Care and Development Innovation Fund

subsection.text: The Child Care and Development Block Grant Act of 1990 (42 U.S.C. 9857 et seq.) is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating section 658P as section 658T, and moving that section 658T to follow section 658S; and
by adding at the end the following:

section.header: Child Care and Development Innovation Fund

subsection.text: The purpose of this section is to—

paragraph.text: improve child care access for parents working hours outside of traditional 9 to 5 work hours, such as parents working an evening, night, or weekend shift; and
address the needs of working parents with young children, so that the parents are able to stay attached to the workforce, attain eligibility for promotions and salary increases, and amass savings.
The term child care program means the child care activities of an eligible child care provider.
The term nontraditional work hours means work hours at least 25 percent of which—

subparagraph.text: are before 9 a.m. or after 5 p.m. on a weekday;
are on a Saturday or Sunday; or
are scheduled within 7 days before required attendance at work for those work hours.

paragraph.text: The term Secretary means the Secretary of Health and Human Services.

Not later than 90 days after the date of enactment of the After Hours Child Care Act, the Secretary shall establish a pilot program, through which the Secretary shall award grants on a competitive basis to eligible entities to pay for the Federal share of the cost of—

subparagraph.text: expanding capacity for an existing (as of January 1, 2027) child care program, including such a program of a family child care provider to serve families in which a parent is working nontraditional work hours;
entering into an enrollment-based contract with—

clause.text: an eligible child care provider to serve such families; or

a fiscal intermediary such as a staffed network of family child care providers, child care resource and referral organization, or entity operating a child care facilities fund for the services of multiple eligible child care providers to serve such families;

subparagraph.text: planning activities, including conducting a needs assessment and outreach to existing eligible child care providers (existing on the date of the outreach);
establishing an onsite child care program at a workplace to serve such families;
expanding capacity for an onsite child care program at a workplace to serve such families; or
establishing a child care program, including a program of a family child care provider with the primary goal of serving such families.

paragraph.text: The Secretary shall award the grant for a period of 5 years. A grant awarded under this section may not be renewed.
The Secretary shall award the grant in an amount of not less than $25,000 and not more than $500,000.

subsection.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under this section, an entity shall be—

paragraph.text: an eligible child care provider; or
a partnership of—

subparagraph.text: an eligible child care provider; and

a lead agency, business, child care resource and referral organization, community development financial institution, staffed network of family child care providers, another intermediary with experience supporting child care providers, or another appropriate entity.

subsection.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under this section, an entity shall submit an application to the Secretary at such time, in such manner, and containing—

paragraph.text: a description of the activities to be funded under the grant;
a description of the objective for the activities, which may be an objective described in a paragraph of subsection (f), including—

subparagraph.text: whether the objective is to increase the quantity or quality of a good or service, specified in the description; and
information on that quantity or quality of that good or service, on the date of submission of the application; and

paragraph.text: the population to be served through the activities.

subsection.text: An entity that receives a grant under this section may use the grant funds for activities that may include—

paragraph.text: staffing the child care program involved;
improving the child care facility and related equipment;
establishing or improving the curriculum of the child care program;
assisting eligible child care providers in meeting health and safety requirements, achieving licensure or registration as a child care provider, or improving quality;
acquiring other items needed for the child care program; and
providing training in the prevention of sudden infant death syndrome and safe sleep practices.

subsection.text: The non-Federal share of the cost described in subsection (c)(1) shall be 25 percent.
Not less often than every 2 years, the Secretary shall prepare and submit to Congress a report that includes—

paragraph.text: information on the number of children served under this section and the employment status of their parents;
general information to demonstrate the impact of activities carried out under grants under this section on child care availability;
for each objective referred to in subsection (e)(2) that is described in an application submitted by a grant recipient, a summary of information obtained by—

subparagraph.text: collecting, from each recipient, information on the quantity or quality of the good or service referred to in subsection (e)(2)(B), as of the last day of the grant period;
comparing the information described in subsection (e)(2)(B) with the information described in subparagraph (A), for each such recipient; and
summarizing that collected information and those comparisons for all the recipients who described the objective in such an application; and

paragraph.text: other information relevant to the grants made under this section.

subsection.text: None of the requirements of this subchapter, other than section 658T, shall apply to this section. No reference in this subchapter to this subchapter shall be considered to include this section.
The Secretary may reserve up to ¼ of 1 percent of the amount appropriated under this subchapter to carry out this section for each of the fiscal years 2027 through 2031.

paragraph.text: Section 658E(c)(2) of the Child Care and Development Block Grant Act of 1990 (42 U.S.C. 9858c(c)(2)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in subparagraph (A)(i)(II), by striking 658P(2) and inserting 658T(2); and
in subparagraph (K)(i)(IV), by striking 658P(6)(B) and inserting 658T(6)(B).

paragraph.text: Section 658K(a)(2) of the Child Care and Development Block Grant Act of 1990 (42 U.S.C. 9858i(a)(2)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in subparagraph (A), by striking 658P(6) and inserting 658T(6); and
in subparagraph (F), by striking 658P(6)(B) and inserting 658T(6)(B).
